structured_payload_candidate.title: Active Endpoints Based on Zone Group Mode
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Active setpoint endpoints for cooling maximum, minimum, and heating maximum vary by Zone Group mode.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone Group mode being Occupied, Cooldown, Setup, Warmup, Setback, or Unoccupied
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set cooling maximum endpoint to Vcool-max except in Warmup/Setback/Unoccupied where it is 0; set minimum endpoint to Vmin except in Cooldown/Setup/Warmup/Setback/Unoccupied where it is 0; set heating maximum endpoint to Vheat-max except in Cooldown/Setup/Setback/Unoccupied where it is 0.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Table 5.11.4 Endpoints  as a Function of Zone Group  Mode
Endpoint  Occupied  Cooldown  Setup  Warmup  Setback  Unoccupied
Cooling maximum  Vcool -max Vcool -max Vcool -max 0 0 0
Minimum  Vmin*  0 0 0 0 0
Heating maximum  Vheat -max 0 0 Vheat -max Vheat -max 0

evidence_quote:
Table 5.11.4 Endpoints  as a Function of Zone Group  Mode
Endpoint  Occupied  Cooldown  Setup  Warmup  Setback  Unoccupied
Cooling maximum  Vcool -max Vcool -max Vcool -max 0 0 0
Minimum  Vmin*  0 0 0 0 0
Heating maximum  Vheat -max 0 0 Vheat -max Vheat -max 0

structured_payload_candidate.title: Airflow Sensor Configuration Selection
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Select control logic based on whether airflow sensors are at both inlets or a single discharge sensor.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
The engineer must select between ventilation logic options:
If there are airflow sensors at both inlets to the box , use Section 5.11.5.1  and delete Section  5.11.5.2 .
If there is a single airflow sensor at the box discharge, use Section 5.11.5.2  and delete Section 5.11.5.1 .

evidence_quote:
The engineer must select between ventilation logic options:
If there are airflow sensors at both inlets to the box , use Section 5.11.5.1  and delete Section  5.11.5.2 .
If there is a single airflow sensor at the box discharge, use Section 5.11.5.2  and delete Section 5.11.5.1 .

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Mode Damper Control with Dual Inlet Airflow Sensors
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: 5.11.5.1 Temperature and Damper Control with Dual Inlet Airflow Sensors
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In cooling state, the cooling-loop resets cold duct airflow setpoint from minimum to cooling maximum; cold duct damper modulates to maintain airflow, hot duct damper closed. If cold-deck SA temp > room temp, limit cold duct setpoint to minimum.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Reset active cold duct airflow setpoint from minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint; modulate cold duct damper to maintain measured cooling airflow at setpoint; close hot duct damper. If cold-deck supply air temperature > room temperature, setpoint shall be no higher than minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
When the Zone State  is cooling , the cooling -loop output shall reset the active cold duct  airflow
setpoint  from the minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint . The cold duct  damper
shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured cooling airflow at the active
cold duct airflow setpoint . The hot duct  damper shall be closed.
1. If cold -deck supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature,
the active cold duct airflow setpoint  shall be no higher than the minimum  endpoint .

evidence_quote:
When the Zone State  is cooling , the cooling -loop output shall reset the active cold duct  airflow
setpoint  from the minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint . The cold duct  damper
shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured cooling airflow at the active
cold duct airflow setpoint . The hot duct  damper shall be closed.
1. If cold -deck supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature,
the active cold duct airflow setpoint  shall be no higher than the minimum  endpoint .

structured_payload_candidate.title: Deadband Mode Damper Control with Dual Inlet Airflow Sensors
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: 5.11.5.1 Temperature and Damper Control with Dual Inlet Airflow Sensors
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In deadband state, airflow setpoints are held at last values before deadband; snap-action switch between ducts.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: When coming from cooling: cold duct airflow setpoint = minimum endpoint, hot duct = 0. When coming from heating: hot duct airflow setpoint = minimum endpoint, cold duct = 0. Dampers maintain those setpoints.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
When the Zone State  is deadband , the active cold duct and hot duct  airflow setpoint s shall be
their last setpoint s just before entering deadband.  In other words, when going from cooling  to
deadband , the active cold duct airflow setpoint  is equal to the minimum  endpoint , and the
active hot duct airflow setpoint  is zero. When going from heating  to deadband , the active hot
duct airflow setpoint  is equal to the minimum  endpoint , and the active cold duct airflow
setpoint  is zero. This results in a snap -action switch in the damper setpoint  as indicated in
Figures 5.11.5 -1 and 5.11.5 -2.

evidence_quote:
When the Zone State  is deadband , the active cold duct and hot duct  airflow setpoint s shall be
their last setpoint s just before entering deadband.  In other words, when going from cooling  to
deadband , the active cold duct airflow setpoint  is equal to the minimum  endpoint , and the
active hot duct airflow setpoint  is zero. When going from heating  to deadband , the active hot
duct airflow setpoint  is equal to the minimum  endpoint , and the active cold duct airflow
setpoint  is zero. This results in a snap -action switch in the damper setpoint  as indicated in
Figures 5.11.5 -1 and 5.11.5 -2.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating Mode Damper Control with Dual Inlet Airflow Sensors
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: 5.11.5.1 Temperature and Damper Control with Dual Inlet Airflow Sensors
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In heating state, heating-loop resets hot duct airflow setpoint from minimum to heating maximum; hot duct damper modulates, cold duct damper closed. If hot-deck SA temp < room temp, limit hot duct setpoint to minimum.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Reset active hot duct airflow setpoint from minimum endpoint to heating maximum endpoint; modulate hot duct damper to maintain measured heating airflow at setpoint; close cold duct damper. If hot-deck supply air temperature < room temperature, setpoint shall be no higher than minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
When the Zone State  is heati ng, the heating -loop output shall reset the active hot duct  airflow
setpoint  from the minimum endpoint to heating maximum endpoint . The hot duct  damper shall
be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured heating airflow at the active hot duct
airflow setpoint . The cold duct  damper shall be closed.
1. If hot -deck supply air temperature from the air handler is less than room temperature, the
active hot duct  airflow setpoint  shall be no higher than the minimum  endpoint .

evidence_quote:
When the Zone State  is heati ng, the heating -loop output shall reset the active hot duct  airflow
setpoint  from the minimum endpoint to heating maximum endpoint . The hot duct  damper shall
be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured heating airflow at the active hot duct
airflow setpoint . The cold duct  damper shall be closed.
1. If hot -deck supply air temperature from the air handler is less than room temperature, the
active hot duct  airflow setpoint  shall be no higher than the minimum  endpoint .

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Mode Damper Control with Single Discharge Airflow Sensor
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: 5.11.5.2 Temperature and Damper Control with a Single Discharge Airflow Sensor
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In cooling state, cooling-loop resets discharge airflow setpoint from minimum to cooling maximum; cold duct damper modulates to maintain discharge airflow, hot duct damper closed.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Reset active discharge airflow setpoint from minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint; modulate cold duct damper to maintain measured discharge airflow at setpoint; close hot duct damper.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
When the Zone State s is cooling , the cooling -loop output shall reset the active discharge
airflow setpoint  from the minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint . The cold duct
damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured discharge airflow at
active discharge airflow setpoint . The hot duct damper shall be closed.

evidence_quote:
When the Zone State s is cooling , the cooling -loop output shall reset the active discharge
airflow setpoint  from the minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint . The cold duct
damper shall be modulated by a control loop to maintain the measured discharge airflow at
active discharge airflow setpoint . The hot duct damper shall be closed.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Deadband Mode Damper Control with Single Discharge Airflow Sensor
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: 5.11.5.2 Temperature and Damper Control with a Single Discharge Airflow Sensor
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In deadband state, discharge airflow setpoint is minimum endpoint, maintained by the damper that was operative before deadband; other damper closed. Snap-action switch.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: When coming from cooling: cold duct damper maintains discharge airflow at minimum endpoint, hot duct damper closed. When coming from heating: hot duct damper maintains discharge airflow at minimum endpoint, cold duct damper closed.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
When the Zone State  is deadband , the active discharge airflow setpoint  shall be the minimum
endpoint , maintained by the damper that was operative just before entering deadband.  The
other damper shall remain closed. In other words, when going from cooling  to deadband , the
cold duct damper shall maintain the discharge airflow at the minimum endpoint , and the hot
duct damper shall be closed. When going from heating  to deadband , the hot duct damper shall
maintain the discharge airflow at the zone minimum endpoint , and the cold duct damper shall
be closed. This results in a snap -action switch in the damp er setpoint  as indicated in Figures
5.11.5 -1 and 5.11.5 -2.

evidence_quote:
When the Zone State  is deadband , the active discharge airflow setpoint  shall be the minimum
endpoint , maintained by the damper that was operative just before entering deadband.  The
other damper shall remain closed. In other words, when going from cooling  to deadband , the
cold duct damper shall maintain the discharge airflow at the minimum endpoint , and the hot
duct damper shall be closed. When going from heating  to deadband , the hot duct damper shall
maintain the discharge airflow at the zone minimum endpoint , and the cold duct damper shall
be closed. This results in a snap -action switch in the damp er setpoint  as indicated in Figures
5.11.5 -1 and 5.11.5 -2.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Override to Prevent Backflow (Heating AHU Not Proven On)
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: 5.11.5.3 Overriding Sections 5.11.5.1 and 5.11.5.2 Logic (to Avoid Backflow from One Duct to the Other)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If heating AHU is not proven ON, close the heating damper.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Heating air handler is not proven ON
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. If heating air handler is not proven ON, the heating damper shall be closed.

evidence_quote: a. If heating air handler is not proven ON, the heating damper shall be closed.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low Airflow Alarm (70% threshold)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate Level 4 alarm if measured airflow < 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Measured airflow less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater
than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.

evidence_quote:
If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater
than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Low Airflow Alarm (50% threshold)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate Level 3 alarm if measured airflow < 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint > 0.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Measured airflow less than 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
If the measured airflow is le ss than 50% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater
than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm.

evidence_quote:
If the measured airflow is le ss than 50% of setpoint  for 10 minutes while setpoint  is greater
than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Suppress Low Airflow Alarms for Zones with Importance-Multiplier = 0
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Low airflow alarms shall be suppressed if zone Importance-Multiplier is 0 for its static pressure reset T&R loop.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Zone has an Importance-Multiplier of 0 for its static pressure reset T&R control loop.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
If a zone has an Importance -Multiplier  of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1 ) for its static pressure
reset T&R  control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.

evidence_quote:
If a zone has an Importance -Multiplier  of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1 ) for its static pressure
reset T&R  control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Airflow Sensor Calibration Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate Level 3 alarm if fan is off and airflow sensor reading exceeds max(10% of max airflow setpoint, 50 cfm) for 30 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Fan serving zone is off and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 30 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
If the fan serving the zone is off and airflow sensor readin g is above
the larger of 10% of the maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 30 minutes , generate a Level 3
alarm.

evidence_quote:
If the fan serving the zone is off and airflow sensor readin g is above
the larger of 10% of the maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 30 minutes , generate a Level 3
alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Leaking Damper Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate Level 4 alarm if damper is closed (0%) and airflow sensor reading exceeds max(10% cooling max setpoint, 50 cfm) for 10 minutes while fan is proven ON.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Damper position is 0%, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while fan serving damper is proven ON.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
If the damper position is 0 %, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of
10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the
damper is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm.

evidence_quote:
If the damper position is 0 %, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of
10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint  or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the
damper is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Custom Thresholds Based on Sensor Accuracy
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The constant value thresholds for airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms should be set based on the minimum accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
The constant value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the
transducer and A/D converter used to measure air flow.  The value used should be determined as the minimum
accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.

evidence_quote:
The constant value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the
transducer and A/D converter used to measure air flow.  The value used should be determined as the minimum
accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Testing/Commissioning Overrides
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Provide software switches to force zone airflow setpoints, damper positions, and reset request-hours accumulators for testing/commissioning.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Provide software switches that interlock to a system level point to force zone airflow setpoint to zero, Vcool-max, Vmin, Vheat-max; force cooling/heating dampers full closed/open; reset request-hours accumulator point to zero (one for each reset type).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
Provide software switches that interlock to a system level point to
a. force  zone airflow setpoint  to zero ,
b. force  zone airflow setpoint  to Vcool -max,
c. force  zone airflow setpoint  to Vmin ,
d. force  zone airflow setpoint  to Vheat -max,
e. force  cooling damper full closed/open ,
f. force  heating damper full closed/open , and
g. reset  request -hours accumulator point to ze ro (provide one point for each reset type listed in the
next section ).

evidence_quote:
Provide software switches that interlock to a system level point to
a. force  zone airflow setpoint  to zero ,
b. force  zone airflow setpoint  to Vcool -max,
c. force  zone airflow setpoint  to Vmin ,
d. force  zone airflow setpoint  to Vheat -max,
e. force  cooling damper full closed/open ,
f. force  heating damper full closed/open , and
g. reset  request -hours accumulator point to ze ro (provide one point for each reset type listed in the
next section ).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling SAT Reset Requests
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate 0–3 requests based on zone temperature above cooling setpoint or Cooling Loop percentage, with suppression after setpoint changes.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone temperature comparing to cooling setpoint; Cooling Loop value
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: If zone temp exceeds cool setpoint by 3°C/5°F for 2 min (after suppression), send 3 requests. Else if exceeds by 2°C/3°F for 2 min (after suppression), send 2 requests. Else if Cooling Loop >95%, send 1 request until <85%. Else send 0 requests.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
5.11.8.1.  Cooling SAT Reset Requests
a. If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint  by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and
after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20 , send 3 requests.
b. Else if the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint  by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes
and after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20 , send 2 requests.
c. Else if the Cooling Loop  is greater than 95%, send 1 request  until the Cooling Loop  is less than
85%.
d. Else if the Cooling Loop  is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

evidence_quote:
5.11.8.1.  Cooling SAT Reset Requests
a. If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint  by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and
after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20 , send 3 requests.
b. Else if the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint  by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes
and after suppression period due to setpoint  change per Section 5.1.20 , send 2 requests.
c. Else if the Cooling Loop  is greater than 95%, send 1 request  until the Cooling Loop  is less than
85%.
d. Else if the Cooling Loop  is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cold-Duct Static Pressure Reset Requests
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate 0–3 requests based on measured airflow deficit at high damper position, with damper position thresholds.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Measured airflow less than setpoint with setpoint > 0 and damper position >95% for 1 minute, or damper position alone
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: If airflow < 50% setpoint, setpoint>0, damper>95% for 1 min → 3 requests. Else if airflow < 70% setpoint, setpoint>0, damper>95% for 1 min → 2 requests. Else if damper>95% → 1 request until <85%. Else 0 requests.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote:
5.11.8.2.  Cold -Duct Static Pressure Reset Requests
a. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint  while setpoint  is greater than zero and the
damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 3 requests.
b. Else if the mea sured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint  while setpoint  is greater than zero and
the damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 2 requests.
c. Else if the damper  position is greater than 95%, send 1 request  until the damper  position is less
than 85%.
d. Else if the damper  position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

evidence_quote:
5.11.8.2.  Cold -Duct Static Pressure Reset Requests
a. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint  while setpoint  is greater than zero and the
damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 3 requests.
b. Else if the mea sured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint  while setpoint  is greater than zero and
the damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 2 requests.
c. Else if the damper  position is greater than 95%, send 1 request  until the damper  position is less
than 85%.
d. Else if the damper  position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
